purpose: Disaggregates the monolithic 'deglobalization' narrative into three separable processes to enable precise analysis of contradictions, timescales, and investment implications
components: Strategic decoupling: deliberate state-directed technology/supply chain severance, 5-15 year horizon, security logic
Tariff-driven fragmentation: episodic, politically motivated protectionism, 1-3 year horizon, mercantilist logic
Resilience restructuring: private-sector risk repricing and supply chain diversification, 3-7 year horizon, corporate logic
current_assessment: All three processes are active simultaneously with frequent contradictions (e.g., US tariffs on Canadian aluminum undermine friendshoring for critical minerals). Using aggregate trade volume as evidence for or against deglobalization is misleading — global goods trade near record $25.3T while compositional shifts are structurally important.
limitations: Classification of specific policies into one process vs another can be ambiguous
Processes interact and can shift between categories over time
Does not capture the relative magnitude or priority of each process
Country-level analysis requires mapping each process separately
